title: A resolution recognizing the 4th anniversary of the Trump administration's Secretary of the Air Force announcing Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama, as the preferred location for United States Space Command Headquarters.
plain_summary: This Senate resolution celebrates the fourth anniversary of when the Trump administration picked Huntsville, Alabama as the best location for Space Command Headquarters. The Air Force studied many locations and followed all the rules when they chose Huntsville. The resolution criticizes President Biden for later changing the decision to Colorado Springs, Colorado, even though that city ranked much lower in the study. The resolution wants the new Trump administration to change the headquarters location back to Huntsville.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Armed Services.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes the fourth anniversary of when the Trump administration's Secretary of the Air Force announced that Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama, was the preferred location for United States Space Command Headquarters.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: On January thirteenth, two thousand and twenty one, the United States Air Force announced that Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama, was the preferred location for United States Space Command Headquarters. January thirteenth, two thousand and twenty five, marks the fourth anniversary of this decision being announced during President Donald Trump's first term. President Trump directed the Secretary of Defense to reestablish United States Space Command as a unified combatant command on December eighteenth, two thousand and eighteen. As a result of President Trump's direction, the Secretary of Defense told the United States Air Force Basing Office to start a process for choosing the preferred permanent location of United States Space Command headquarters, called the two thousand and nineteen Basing Action. The Secretary of the Air Force signed a memorandum approving a temporary headquarters while waiting for the selection of a preferred permanent location for United States Space Command on January fifteenth, two thousand and twenty. On April fifteenth, two thousand and nineteen, the Secretary of the Air Force was given the authority to make a decision on the preferred permanent location for United States Space Command Headquarters. As a further result of President Trump's direction, the Secretary of Defense reestablished United States Space Command as a unified combatant command on August twenty ninth, two thousand and nineteen. Because Congress had concerns about the two thousand and nineteen Basing Action, the Secretary of Defense and Secretary of the Air Force met to discuss a modified process and approved the two thousand and twenty Basing Action, called the Strategic Basing Action, on April twenty seventh, two thousand and twenty. The approved Strategic Basing Action had three phases and was started on May fourteenth, two thousand and twenty, beginning with the Self Nomination Phase and asking for proposals from military installations interested in hosting United States Space Command Headquarters. During the Self Nomination Phase, sixty six candidate locations in twenty six States were nominated and the Air Force Basing Office determined fifty locations met the initial requirements. The second Strategic Basing Action phase, called the Evaluation Phase, began on July twenty third, two thousand and twenty, and assessed each of the fifty locations on four evaluation factors called Mission, Capacity, Community, and Costs to the Department of Defense, with twenty one criteria between the factors. The Evaluation Phase concluded on November eighteenth, two thousand and twenty, and the United States Air Force publicly announced six finalists to advance to the third phase of the Strategic Basing Action on November nineteenth, two thousand and twenty, ranked in the following order. First was Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama. Second was Offutt Air Force Base in Bellevue, Nebraska. Third was Joint Base San Antonio in San Antonio, Texas. Fourth was Peterson Air Force Base in Colorado Springs, Colorado. Fifth was Kirtland Air Force Base in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Sixth was Patrick Air Force Base in Cape Canaveral, Florida. The two thousand and twenty two Department of Defense Inspector General report titled Evaluation of the Air Force Selection Process for the Permanent Location of the U.S. Space Command Headquarters found that there was a large break in qualification that occurred after the top two locations during the Evaluation Phase. The third phase of the Strategic Basing Action, called the Selection Phase, was conducted from December fourth, two thousand and twenty, through January seventh, two thousand and twenty one. The findings of the Selection Phase resulted in the six finalists from the Evaluation Phase being ranked in the following order. First was Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama. Second was Kirtland Air Force Base in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Third was Offutt Air Force Base in Bellevue, Nebraska. Fourth was Joint Base San Antonio in San Antonio, Texas. Fifth was Peterson Air Force Base in Colorado Springs, Colorado. Sixth was Patrick Air Force Base in Cape Canaveral, Florida. The Strategic Basing Action found that Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama, consistently ranked as the top location throughout the process and compared more favorably across the four key factors and twenty one criteria than any other finalist location. The Strategic Basing Action found that Huntsville, more so than any other finalist location, provided a large, qualified workforce, quality schools, superior infrastructure, and low initial and recurring costs to the Department of Defense. The aforementioned points proved that Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama, was and remains to be, the best location for United States Space Command Headquarters and informed the decision of the United States Air Force selecting the site as the preferred location on January thirteenth, two thousand and twenty one. The findings of the Strategic Basing Action have been supported by reports following subsequent Department of Defense Inspector General and Government Accountability Office investigations. Despite an extensive process determining that Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama, was the best possible location for United States Space Command, on July thirty first, two thousand and twenty three, President Joseph Biden and his Presidential administration chose to disregard the findings of the Strategic Basing Action and announced the intention to locate the headquarters at the fifth best location, Colorado Springs, Colorado.

sections.title: Senate Resolution
sections.text: The Senate recognizes that the United States Air Force's Strategic Basing Action process complied with law and policy and was justified in identifying Huntsville as the preferred permanent location for United States Space Command Headquarters. The Senate strongly commends President Donald Trump and his first Presidential administration for completing a robust and fact based Strategic Basing Action focused on what was best to ensure the national security and fiscal responsibility of the United States and well being of service members and their families. The Senate strongly condemns President Joseph Biden and his Presidential administration for disregarding the findings of the Strategic Basing Action and allowing United States Space Command Headquarters to be based at the fifth best location. The Senate encourages President Donald Trump and his incoming second Presidential administration to halt the Biden administration's disastrous decision and immediately proceed in establishing a permanent headquarters for United States Space Command at Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, Alabama.
